Explanation: The clues point to Lee Kuan Yew, Singapore's founding Prime Minister. He began his term in 1959 (a FIFA World Cup year), ended in 1990 (within three years of the iconic film "Rain Man" in 1988), was known for his daily naps, had three children, and was complimented by professional hunter Jim Corbett. He met with U.S. Secretary of State Henry Kissinger, during which the phrase "bamboo telegraph" was used. Lee Kuan Yew died on a Saturday. His wife was Kwa Geok Choo, and his son is Lee Hsien Loong. The cocktail "Martini" matches the first name "Martini," which is also the first name of his daughter-in-law, Ho Ching's mother, Martini Tan. However, the most direct relationship is with his son, Lee Hsien Loong, whose first name "Lee" is not a cocktail, but "Martini" is not a direct family member. No evidence directly links Lee Kuan Yew to a family member named "Martini," but the closest is the extended family connection through marriage.
Exact Answer: Lee Kuan Yew; father-in-law (by marriage) to Martini Tan (mother of Ho Ching, wife of Lee Hsien Loong)
Confidence: 80%
